judge_result.judge:
@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent implemented a correct plan (collect OrderItem IDs for the product and count cases by issue over the last five months) but, as shown in its final SQL built in execute_python and run against support ("ltrim(orderitemid__c, '#') IN (...)"), it only stripped leading "#" and did not trim trailing whitespace from orderitemid__c, causing missed matches and an incorrect top issue ID versus the ground truth.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After retrieving the relevant OrderItem, Order, Account, and Case data, the agent incorrectly mapped "AI Cirku-Tech" to the wrong account ("Circuit Dynamics Inc.") in execute_python (function-call-6720163986754879831), thereby computing the most frequent issue for the wrong account and returning a03Wt00000JqmX6IAJ instead of the correct a03Wt00000JqnHwIAJ.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: yes
FM4: no
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After correctly fetching cases for the product and period and computing issue counts, the agent observed a three-way tie (var_function-call-15969748883425309318) but then arbitrarily selected one with Counter.most_common(1) (var_function-call-2793457905352015440), leading to the wrong issue ID.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent queried support cases with only a lower date bound (createddate >= '2022-08-16' in function-call-7960583469054488810), omitting the upper bound tied to the given "today" (2023-01-16), so cases beyond the five-month window influenced the counts and led it to select a03Wt00000JqzSfIAJ instead of the correct a03Wt00000JqnHwIAJ.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent filtered cases only by date and OrderItem IDs for the product, but never restricted to the AI Cirku-Tech account (nor bounded the date to the five-month window), so it counted issues across all accounts and returned a03Wt00000JqmX6IAJ instead of the account-specific most frequent issue a03Wt00000JqnHwIAJ, as shown in the trace where support."Case" was queried without an account filter and then aggregated.
B. Failure modes encountered:
